digest.greeting: Good evening, Jean. Friday closed with the EPMO EOW readout and the CoE Portal refresh triage marked complete, while the remaining carry-forward items stay focused on Salesforce and pipeline decisions, Legal Change Management follow-through, and the Latent Variables reply. Microsoft 365 enrichment was rate-limited during this run, so this digest stays grounded in the dashboard and morning brief.

items.label: Prepared the EPMO EOW program readout
items.detail: The EOW check-in prep item was marked complete for today.

items.label: Triaged the CoE Portal refresh issue
items.detail: The failed CoE Portal refresh follow-up was marked complete for today.

items.label: Lock Salesforce and pipeline decisions from the afternoon stack
items.detail: Carry forward prioritization changes, BA assignment needs, UAT status, and anything that still belongs in the executive deck after the Salesforce standup, project pipeline sync, and slide update.

items.label: Reply to Yash on the Latent Variables platform questions
items.detail: Yash offered to answer questions by email or set a 20-minute call, with availability today and tomorrow in Pacific time. Decide whether to handle this asynchronously or schedule a call.

items.label: Clear Legal Change Management IT/EPMO questions
items.detail: Carry forward any remaining doubts, action items, or clarifications from the morning IT/EPMO sync so they do not spill too far into next week.

items.label: Monday starts with timecards and Legal Change Management
items.detail: Approve Timecards & Prepare for the Week is at 8:00 AM, followed by Legal Change Management at 10:00 AM.

items.label: Amazon Connect Venn UAT Phase II is the main readiness anchor
items.detail: The 11:30 AM session is scoped to environment readiness, Phase II test scenarios, tester access, phone numbers, configuration, schedule, and support process.

items.label: Afternoon portfolio reviews continue
items.detail: BD Portfolio Review and the Maria 1:1 both name roadblocks, portfolio status, and next steps as useful prep areas.
